commit.message:
Desaturate hair sprites to greyscale for improved color tinting

Convert 1628 hair PNGs under LPC/head/hair*/ from colored originals to
bright greyscale (60-255 range) with per-image levels stretch. This makes
SpriteRenderer.color tinting produce vivid, clearly visible hair colors
while preserving all original shading detail.

feat: extend explain plan with pipeline stages and distributed fan-out

Pipeline stages: Each pipe operator (stats, where, eval, sort, etc.) now
appears in the explain response with execution mode (streaming /
materializing / short-circuit / coordinator-only / render-hint) and a
human-readable note explaining what the operator does and how the engine
will execute it.

Distributed explain: The Explain handler now fans out ForwardExplain RPCs
to remote nodes, collects their ChunkPlan entries (with node_id set), and
merges them into the response. Users see the full cross-node execution
plan instead of only local chunks.

commit.message:
Fix Reviews page 500: remove replies JOIN, fetch separately

The embedded replies JOIN (with deleted_at=is.null filter) caused
PostgREST to generate an inefficient lateral join that timed out
(13s → 500) due to ~400K soft-deleted rows in the replies table.

Fix:
1. Remove replies from main fetchReviews select (no JOIN)
2. Fetch reviews with specific columns only (exclude large 'raw' JSONB)
3. Fetch replies separately via IN (review_ids) using idx_replies_review_id
   partial index (WHERE deleted_at IS NULL) — fast and correct
4. Merge replies client-side per review_id

Also added ANALYZE to cleanup_deleted_replies() to keep query planner
statistics fresh during the ongoing cleanup of ~400K soft-deleted rows.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Change MODEM_RATE from 9600 to 8000 Hz to match telephony standard

slmodemd's DSP was running at 9600 Hz (matching the STLC7550 hardware
codec), but our audio path goes through Asterisk which operates at
8000 Hz. This caused a sample rate mismatch — modem tones were garbled
and no handshake could complete. 8000 Hz is the PSTN standard rate and
the modem DSP algorithms support it natively.

commit.message:
Replace per-action llm_input with context_entry_count to fix large session storage

llm_input stored ~220KB of redundant prompt data per action, causing JSONB rows
to exceed limits on sessions with 400+ actions (silent write failures in after()).
The system prompt and context entries are already stored once per session — only
store the entry count per action. Keep action and raw_response at full size.

commit.message:
Fix 'Something went wrong' when adding student to registration

Three bugs fixed:

1. admin.html: `querySelector('button:last-child')` matched the remove
   button nested inside `.stu-badge` spans (which are :last-child of
   their parent span), not the direct-child '+ Add Student' button.
   `insertBefore(badge, wrongRef)` then threw a DOMException caught by
   the generic catch block, showing 'Something went wrong'. Fixed in all
   three badge-injection sites by using `':scope > button'` to restrict
   to direct children only.

2. backend/main.py delete_student: Deleting a student with a pending
   (non-finalized) registration caused a FK constraint violation (500).
   Now clears EventRegistrationStudent rows first before deleting.

3. backend/main.py verify_payment: For the add-students flow the reg is
   already finalized before payment, so the early-return guard prevented
   new students from being added from Stripe session metadata. Rewrote
   to always retrieve the Stripe session and branch on whether
   new_student_ids / observer_ids metadata is present.

commit.message:
Security hardening: CSRF protection, API key hashing, input validation, SMTP audit

- CSRF: double-submit cookie pattern on all mutating endpoints (skip webhooks/HMAC, Bearer auth, auth entry points)
- API keys: SHA-256 hashed storage, keyPrefix for identification, 60 req/min rate limit per key
- Input validation: path traversal fix in serveStatic (path.resolve check), parseInt radix
- SMTP relay: audited and marked complete (plan-gated, domain-scoped IAM, SES rate limits)
- Migration script for existing plaintext API keys: scripts/migrate-api-keys-hash.ts
- Graceful CSRF rollout: /api/auth/me issues CSRF cookie to existing sessions

commit.message:
TICKET-103: Build-time version hash and version polling (#105)

* TICKET-103: Create branch and epic/ticket tracking

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* TICKET-103: Build-time version hash and version polling

Add client-side version detection to automatically detect when a new
frontend build has been deployed. Injects git SHA as __APP_VERSION__ at
build time, generates version.json on deploy with no-cache headers, and
provides a polling module (versionCheck.ts) that compares versions and
notifies subscribers on mismatch.

fix(docker): externalize WASM packages and fix CRS path for non-root user (#216)

Externalize WASM packages to fix runtime loading. Bake CRS_PATH into
Dockerfiles so bb.js can cache its Common Reference String data in a
directory owned by the app user, instead of failing on the non-existent
home directory.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: wire chatbot live context to actual regime classifier pipeline (#6)

The chatbot was querying an empty regime_states table instead of using
the same classifier pipeline as the dashboard, causing it to always
report "unknown" regime. Now fetch_live_context() uses
get_latest_features() + MLRegimeClassifier (with rule-based fallback),
matching exactly what the dashboard endpoint computes.

- Rewrite fetch_live_context() to mirror /api/v1/regime/current logic
- Add VIX level, volatility regime, and regime probabilities to context
- Compute recent transitions from actual CISS/VIX data (not empty table)
- Reduce context cache TTL from 15min to 2min
- Update system prompt to tell Claude the context is live dashboard data
- Update Python schemas and TypeScript types to match enriched context

commit.message:
B-tree and hash column indexes (#28)

* docs: documentation uplift design — README, ADRs, rustdocs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: documentation uplift implementation plan

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: B-tree and hash column index design

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: B-tree/hash index implementation plan

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: BTreeIndex + HashIndex — column indexes with incremental Changed<T> updates

Add two generic secondary index structs to the index module:

- BTreeIndex<T: Component + Ord + Clone> — BTreeMap-backed, supports
  exact match (get) and ordered range queries (range).
- HashIndex<T: Component + Hash + Eq + Clone> — HashMap-backed,
  supports exact match (get).

Both implement SpatialIndex with incremental update via Changed<T>
(only re-indexes entities whose component mutated since last call).
Stale entries after despawn are handled lazily via is_alive filtering,
cleaned up on next rebuild.

Exported from crate root alongside SpatialIndex. 10 new tests covering
rebuild, range, empty, incremental update, stale entries, multi-archetype,
duplicate values, and trait object satisfaction.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: index example — B-tree range queries and hash exact lookups

Adds BTreeIndex<T> and HashIndex<T> to crates/minkowski/src/index.rs as
concrete SpatialIndex implementations. BTreeIndex supports ordered range
queries; HashIndex supports O(1) exact lookups. Both use Changed<T> for
incremental updates and lazy stale-entry detection via generational
entity validation.

The example (examples/examples/index.rs) demonstrates building indexes,
range queries, exact lookups, incremental update after mutations, and
stale entry detection after despawn.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: ADR-011 + CLAUDE.md/README updates for column indexes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: per-index ChangeTick replaces shared Changed<T> query cache

Indexes now store their own ChangeTick and use World::query_changed_since()
instead of world.query::<Changed<T>>(). This avoids the shared query cache
tick — two indexes on the same component can both call update() sequentially
and both see the same changes.

- Add public ChangeTick type (opaque wrapper around internal Tick)
- Add World::change_tick() and World::query_changed_since::<T>()
- BTreeIndex/HashIndex store per-instance last_sync tick
- New test: two_indexes_same_component_independent_update

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: get_valid/range_valid + World::has for component-removal safety

Component removal leaves stale entries in indexes (entity alive but
component detached). New validated query methods filter these:

- World::has::<T>(entity) — archetype bitset check, no data touched
- BTreeIndex::get_valid / range_valid — filters despawns + removals
- HashIndex::get_valid — same

Two lookup tiers: raw `get` for hot paths, `get_valid` for safety.
Stale memory reclaimed on next rebuild.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: review findings — sparse guard, doc accuracy, test coverage

- debug_assert! in query_changed_since for sparse components
- Fix doc: (Entity, T) not (Entity, &T), clarify column granularity
- Fix Changed<T> conflation in ADR-011, SpatialIndex doc, example header
- World::has doc: mention sparse path alongside bitset check
- ChangeTick doc: note Default semantics (tick 0 = see all changes)
- Add ChangeTick to CLAUDE.md pub list
- 9 new tests: World::has (5) + World::query_changed_since (4)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: len/contains methods + tests for range_valid and hash get_valid

- BTreeIndex/HashIndex: add len(), is_empty(), contains(entity)
- Test range_valid with component removal filtering
- Test HashIndex::get_valid with component removal filtering
- Test len/contains on both index types

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
v0.7.1: per-center differential FP, Atlas v6/v7 results

- Add per_center_differential_fingerprint() for isolated per-center
  chirality signals. Returns list[np.ndarray], one FP per center.
- Atlas v6 results: global diff FP Spearman rho=-0.536, Pearson r=-0.666
  (p=0.018). Anti-correlation range -0.09 to -0.93 for 11 chiral pairs.
- Atlas v7 results: per-center FP did NOT improve correlation (rho=-0.476).
  Divergence saturation is the bottleneck, not multi-center cancellation.
- Version bump 0.7.0 -> 0.7.1
